title: pedicularis cephalantha var. typica
intro: Pedicularis cephalantha var. typica, also known as the Western lousewort, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of western North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pedicularis cephalantha var. typica include relieving symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, with the roots and leaves being the most commonly used parts.
